title: Improvements to the quest system
body:
# Improvements to the quest system

## Goal

Root quest for all improvements to the quest system itself — the writ type, workflow, skill, tooling, vocabulary, and orientation rituals. Children may be active design threads, CLI prereqs, naming/vocabulary debates, or infrastructure gaps. This root exists so the quest-about-quests work has a stable anchor rather than scattering across the board.

## Status

Active. Children represent the current set of in-flight and pending improvements. See Next Steps for the aggregate state of the sub-tree.

## Next Steps

**Aggregate state of children (keep this honest — this is where the anti-hiding discipline lives):**

- `w-mnswvmj7` **Initial implementation and MVP** — sub-umbrella (formerly "Writ substrate & the quest/topic writ type"). V1 of the quest writ type shipped; one T1.x child still open under it: T1.4 structured decisions/ratification `w-mnswwzdv`. Renamed and re-parented here 2026-04-15 — scopes "what shipped and what v1-era substrate work remains" under this root.
- `w-mnt106rv` Quest-helper CLI wrapper for nsg — re-parented here 2026-04-15 at Sean's direction. Root-direct for now; could reasonably be pulled under the MVP sub-umbrella if we want substrate-shaped items to cluster there. Flag for the next triage.
- `w-mnt0i31e` Event-log layer for quest writs — re-parented here 2026-04-15 at Sean's direction. Same disposition note as `w-mnt106rv`.
- `w-mo0ffnhw` Use top-level quests as focusing tool — **active design**, open questions awaiting Sean's decisions. Has three CLI children forming a query/mutate/present set (`w-mo0ffwjw` filter, `w-mo0ffwwc` re-parent, `w-mo0gi7td` tree index artifact). This is the live workflow thread on this root.
- `w-mo0gias9` **Pilgrimage: explore alternative storage for quests beyond writs** — exploratory quest opened 2026-04-15. Questions whether the writ substrate is still the right home for quests given real friction (Oculus UI, CLI vocabulary, status types). Explicitly not a decision quest — output is a written assessment, not a migration. Framed as a pilgrimage per the vocabulary tome. Probably low priority until the friction feels acute or a large writ-substrate change is contemplated.
- `w-mnsx3onr` Rename 'quest' — parked vocabulary debate. Previously parented under `w-mnswvmj7` (before rename), re-parented directly here 2026-04-15 at Sean's direction. No forcing function yet; worth keeping open because the word-fit question is real and low-stakes parking is the right shape for it.

**Not a candidate** (surfaced during review but deliberately kept elsewhere):

- `w-mnsx9lbz` Parent-child relationship semantics — concerns all writs, not just quests. Correctly parented under the framework writ-substrate root `w-mnsx8cz2`, even though its topic touches how quest restructuring feels. Cross-referenced for context.
- `w-mnsz2ku6` Work-tracking primitives beyond writs+quests (backlog/seeds/threads) — adjacent: asks whether to add new primitives, which would reshape this umbrella. Keep as its own top-level for now; revisit if the decision goes in favor of new primitives.
- `w-mnzhdub5` Rethink commission-log granularity — primarily about the commission-log data file, not the quest system.
- `w-mnsz1s1x`, `w-mnszc0r5`, `w-mnsz0ve6` — the "explicit contracts" triad. Cross-cutting principle quests; they happen to touch conversation style and agent interaction, not quest machinery. Keep separate.

When any of these children moves, this Next Steps section should be updated to reflect the new aggregate shape. If this section is stale, the root is hiding rather than focusing.

## Context

Why a dedicated root for quest-system improvements:

- Quest-about-quest work has been scattering across the board since April 10 — some items are about the skill, some about the writ substrate, some about the vocabulary, some about the CLI. A single anchor makes the shape visible.
- The current flat orientation model (scan all quests at startup) is the specific problem that motivated this restructuring. This root is the place where the fix to that problem — the top-level focusing tool — gets designed and tracked.
- Framework-level writ substrate work (multi-rig, writ-status model, parent-child semantics across all writs) belongs to the writ-substrate root, not here. This root is scoped to the *quest writ type* and its surrounding skill/workflow, not to the general writ machinery.

## References

- Sibling root (framework writ substrate): `w-mnsx8cz2` — Writs as obligations, rigs as attempts.
- Child sub-umbrella: `w-mnswvmj7` — Initial implementation and MVP (formerly "Writ substrate & the quest/topic writ type"). Holds the T1.x substrate children.
- Related (completed): `w-mnsww5kg` plugin-contributed writ types, `w-mnt0jin1` file-canonical quest bodies, `w-mnsx90p1` writ status model simplification. These define the substrate this root builds on.
- Related (cancelled): `w-mnshgkja` quest-scribe subagent — cancelled because the gating condition for context-pressure optimization didn't trigger.
- Quests skill: `.claude/skills/quests/SKILL.md` — the workflow this root governs.

## Notes

- 2026-04-15: opened during the quest-rollout triage session (Coco session 166741dd). Created as anchor for restructuring quest-system work after Sean grouped related items into parent/child structure and proposed a top-level focusing-tool orientation model.

items.title: Pilgrimage: explore alternative storage for quests beyond writs
Rename 'quest' — the word doesn't sit right
